app.name: Feluda
app.one_liner: A Rust-based command-line tool that analyzes project dependencies, records their licenses, and flags restrictive or incompatible usage permissions.

screens.name: License Compliance Report
screens.purpose: Display analyzed dependencies with their licenses and compliance warnings.
primary_actions: Configure GitHub token for authenticated API access

rust_commands.name: analyze_dependencies
rust_commands.purpose: Scan project dependencies across multiple ecosystems and determine license compliance status.

rust_commands.name: check_local_licenses
rust_commands.purpose: Inspect local dependency manifests (Cargo.toml, go.mod, requirements.txt, etc.) for embedded license info.

rust_commands.name: fetch_github_license
rust_commands.purpose: Retrieve license information from GitHub API with optional token authentication and caching.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add basic CLI output formatting for compliance warnings
week 1: Implement core dependency parsing for Rust (Cargo) and Go (go.mod)
week 1: Integrate GitHub license fetching with caching
acceptance_tests: Given a Go module with a local LICENSE file, when Feluda scans it offline, then it should detect the license without calling GitHub API.
Given a project with a dependency under GPL-3.0, when Feluda runs with a valid --github-token, then it correctly identifies the license and flags incompatibility if the project uses MIT.
Given a Rust project with a permissive license, when Feluda analyzes it, then it should report no restrictive licenses.
